# AgentPassport — Email skill

> Owned-domain inboxes for AI agents — receive mail, send mail.

This is the **email** capability of AgentPassport. Provision real inboxes on
a domain the user owns, receive every inbound message as the full raw RFC 5322
(headers + body, exactly as the sender delivered it), send transactional mail
from custom addresses. The agent parses whatever it cares about — subject,
body, codes, links, HTML. No server-side regex. Agent-first: JSON output,
deterministic exit codes, no interactive prompts at runtime.

NOT passport.js (Node auth middleware). NOT an AI-identity protocol. NOT
temp-mail — every address is bound to a domain the user owns via DNS delegation.

## Install

```
npm install -g @agentpassport/cli
```

## Authenticate

```
export AP_API_KEY=ap_live_xxxxxxxx   # or ap_test_* for the dev environment
```

Key prefix decides the environment:

```
ap_live_*  →  api.agentpassport.sh        (production)
ap_test_*  →  dev.api.agentpassport.sh    (internal / dev)
```

Get a live key at https://agentpassport.sh after sign-up.

## Prerequisite (human, once per domain)

```
app domain add myagent.com
# returns 2 nameservers; user copies them to their registrar
# (GoDaddy / Porkbun / Cloudflare / etc.). 5–30 min propagation.
```

## Common tasks (agent-runnable)

- Provision an inbox (first call on a fresh domain auto-configures MX/SPF/catch_all):
  `app email create --domain myagent.com --name support`
- Wait for the next message at an inbox (msg.raw has the full RFC 5322):
  `app email watch --inbox support@myagent.com --timeout 60s --json`
- Read the latest N messages (newest first):
  `app email read --inbox support@myagent.com -n 10 --json`
- Send mail from the inbox (post-MVP):
  `app email send --from support@myagent.com --to b@y.com --subject "..." --body "..."`

Every inbound message has shape `{ id, to, receivedAt, raw }` — `raw` is the
full RFC 5322 source. To pull out a verification code, read raw and grep it
yourself (or hand it to the LLM and ask):
  `code=$(app email watch --inbox ... --json | jq -r .raw | grep -oE '\b[0-9]{4,8}\b' | head -1)`

## Other AgentPassport capabilities (separate skills, post-MVP)

- `agentpassport-proxy` — residential IP proxies (not yet shipped)
- `agentpassport-vpn` — WireGuard VPN tunnels (not yet shipped)

These will trigger as their own skills when shipped — Claude Code's skill
router picks one based on the user's request, you don't load all of them
at once.
